title: How to Use the Compatibility Utility
breadcrumbs: How to Use the Compatibility Utility
text: How to Use the Compatibility Utility The Compatibility Utility consists of JavaScript modules located in the default installation directory: uniface/webapps/ux_compatibility_utils/ . This utility ensures compatibility of the UX Widget API's data arguments with the previous interface version. The exported module includes a set of functions that convert the data arguments in UX Widget API functions to the format required by the previous interface version. The modules are named to indicate the interface version compatibility they support (e.g., v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js for converting from Interface Version 2 to Version 1). Each function is named to clearly indicate its purpose within the corresponding Widget API. The example below refers to the module exported in v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js . The following Utility functions are available as part of the v2_to_v1 export: dataUpdate_data(data) Converts the data argument of the dataUpdate() function to the old format. dataCleanup_dataNames(dataNames) Converts the dataNames argument of the dataCleanup() function to the old format. processLayout_objectDefinition(objectDefinition) Converts an array of property names from the objectDefinition argument of the processLayout() function to the old format. onConnect_objectDefinition(objectDefinition) Converts an array of property names from the objectDefinition argument of the onConnect() function to the old format. Developers can import the module and invoke the utility functions in the appropriate Widget’s API function inside the widget class implementations. For example: Explain this code // Source file - mywidget.js import { v2_to_v1 } from '../../ux_compatibility_utils/v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js'; // import compatibility utility module from 'ux_compatibility_utils' folder located in the web root location processLayout(skeletonWidgetElement, objectDefinition) { // invoke utility function for processLayout to convert the objectDefinition argument to previous format objectDefinition = v2_to_v1.processLayout_objectDefinition(objectDefinition); // Existing code remains the same ... } Copy Once the widget class implementation is updated to invoke the utility functions from the respective API functions, the custom widget will be ready to work with newer UX Interface version The next step is to update the physical widget configuration with the with the appropriate version declaration, e.g., uxInterfaceVersion=2 . This indicates that the widget is implemented or adapted to work with Interface Version 2. Related Links processLayout A function that informs a widget to provide a processed initial HTML layout to be used at a run-time. onConnect Initializes the current widget when it is connected to a Document Object Model (DOM) element. dataUpdate Updates a widget's state when the data object's data is changed. dataCleanup Cleans up the widget when it is unbound from a data object.
